stories.title: Breaking: US CPI inflation holds steady at 2.4% in February as forecast
stories.summary: US annual CPI held at 2.4% in February, matching expectations, while the core gauge rose 2.5% year over year and markets reassessed the Federal Reserve outlook.

stories.title: USD: Fed cuts repriced on higher inflation – ING
stories.summary: ING said Fed easing expectations for 2026 have been reduced as firmer inflation and resilient growth push likely rate cuts later into the year.

stories.title: Fed: Gradual cuts and steady inflation path – Danske Bank
stories.summary: Danske Bank expects US growth to cool modestly, inflation to remain contained near the mid-2% range, and the Fed to cut twice before holding steady.

stories.title: US: Above-potential growth and steady Fed stance – BNP Paribas
stories.summary: BNP Paribas sees US growth above potential in 2026 with inflation still above target, keeping the Fed on hold after prior cuts.

stories.title: Federal Reserve set to pause after three consecutive interest-rate cuts
stories.summary: Markets widely expected the Federal Reserve to hold rates steady after three cuts, with focus turning to Chair Powell's guidance.

stories.title: USD: Fed patience tempers 2026 easing hopes – Deutsche Bank
stories.summary: Deutsche Bank said patient Fed rhetoric and sticky inflation have slightly reduced market expectations for additional rate cuts in 2026.

stories.title: USD: Fed cuts and softer inflation outlook – MUFG
stories.summary: MUFG said stronger jobs data reduce immediate easing pressure, but slowing inflation still leaves room for Fed cuts and a weaker dollar outlook.

stories.title: The Private Credit Collapse Accelerates
stories.summary: Private credit stress intensified as redemption pressure rose and concerns spread across leveraged and illiquid segments of the market.
